title: Bitwise operators should be avoided in boolean contexts
description: Bitwise operators should be avoided in boolean contextsAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEpitfallWhy is this an issue?Using bitwise operators (&, |, ^, ~, <<, >>, and >>>) in boolean contexts is likely a mistake. The logical alternatives (&&, ||, !, and comparisons) should be used instead.In the context of a boolean expression, the bitwise operator will return an integer and then cast it to a boolean, evaluating 1 as true and 0 as false. Specifically:when used with negative integers, the bitwise operators will convert the value via two's complement, which leads to unintuitive resultswhen used with floating point numbers, the
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Use logical operators (&&, ||) instead of bitwise operators (&, |) in boolean contexts.
